title: Beyond Words
anchor_summary: A single-player word puzzle/strategy game built around Scrabble-like tile play, run-based progression, stacking modifiers, boss challenges, and score-chasing roguelike synergies.

similar_games.title: Letter Quest: Grimm's Journey
similar_games.why_similar: Word-building is the core action, wrapped in run progression, upgrades, and combo-driven tactical decision-making.

similar_games.title: Balatro
similar_games.why_similar: Not a word game, but very close in its roguelike score-chasing structure, build-defining modifiers, and synergy-heavy runs.

similar_games.title: Bookworm Adventures Deluxe
similar_games.why_similar: Uses word construction as the primary play loop while layering in progression, encounter structure, and strategic letter use.

similar_games.title: SpellTower
similar_games.why_similar: A recognizable modern word-puzzle comp focused on board management, scoring efficiency, and adapting to evolving tile states.

similar_games.title: Word Forward
similar_games.why_similar: Another word-focused puzzle game that emphasizes chaining words, clever board manipulation, and high-score optimization.

title: The Occultist
anchor_summary: A first-person survival-horror adventure centered on tense exploration, stealthy enemy avoidance, environmental puzzle-solving, and occult investigation using a special paranormal tool rather than direct combat.

similar_games.title: Amnesia: The Dark Descent
similar_games.why_similar: First-person horror built around exploration, puzzles, and hiding from supernatural threats instead of fighting back.

similar_games.title: Outlast
similar_games.why_similar: Another first-person horror game focused on vulnerable stealth, escape, and survival through hostile encounters with no real combat.

similar_games.title: Visage
similar_games.why_similar: Shares slow-burn first-person haunted exploration, oppressive atmosphere, puzzle interaction, and lethal supernatural entities.

similar_games.title: MADiSON
similar_games.why_similar: A first-person occult horror experience emphasizing investigation, environmental puzzles, and interaction with paranormal forces.

similar_games.title: Call of Cthulhu
similar_games.why_similar: Close match on first-person occult investigation and story-driven exploration, though it leans more into detective/RPG structure than pure stealth horror.

title: Samson
anchor_summary: Open-world action game built around close-quarters brawling and aggressive driving, with mission-based progression, city pressure systems, and cars used as combat tools rather than just transport.

similar_games.title: Sleeping Dogs
similar_games.why_similar: Open-world urban action centered on hand-to-hand combat, city traversal, missions, and frequent driving between fights.

similar_games.title: Mad Max
similar_games.why_similar: Blends brutal melee combat with weaponized vehicular encounters, upgrade progression, and hostile open-world districts.

similar_games.title: Wheelman
similar_games.why_similar: Arcade-leaning city action focused on chaining driving stunts, chases, crashes, and combat in mission-driven structure.

similar_games.title: Grand Theft Auto IV
similar_games.why_similar: Story-led open-world crime game with driving-heavy mission flow, police pressure, and a grounded urban tone.

similar_games.title: Yakuza 0
similar_games.why_similar: Dense city action with brawler combat, progression upgrades, and story-driven street conflicts, though driving is far less central.

title: Starfield - Terran Armada
anchor_summary: Story DLC for Starfield, so the clearest comps are the base game first, then single-player open-world first-person RPGs with heavy questing, loot/progression, shooting, exploration, and faction-driven content.

similar_games.title: Starfield
similar_games.why_similar: Terran Armada directly inherits Starfield’s core loop: first-person space RPG questing, shooting, exploration, gear progression, and faction/story content.

similar_games.title: Fallout 4
similar_games.why_similar: Bethesda-style first-person open-world RPG with quest hubs, loot, crafting, gunplay, companions, and exploration-driven progression.

similar_games.title: The Outer Worlds
similar_games.why_similar: Single-player sci-fi action RPG focused on dialogue quests, first-person combat, character builds, companions, and hub-based planetary exploration.

similar_games.title: Cyberpunk 2077
similar_games.why_similar: Narrative-heavy first-person RPG with shooter combat, gear/build progression, side quests, and a strong story-expansion style content model.

similar_games.title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
similar_games.why_similar: Another Bethesda open-world RPG built around freeform exploration, faction questlines, character progression, and sandbox-style story discovery.

title: Machine Child
anchor_summary: A daughter-raising life sim centered on scheduling activities, building affection, triggering many story events, customizing appearance, and steering the child toward multiple endings based on your choices.

similar_games.title: Princess Maker 2 Regeneration
similar_games.why_similar: The clearest match: a classic daughter-raising sim where you schedule study, work, leisure, and social activities to shape stats, relationships, and branching endings.

similar_games.title: Volcano Princess
similar_games.why_similar: Modern princess-raising sim focused on nurturing a daughter through daily planning, outfit changes, events, affection, and many possible futures.

similar_games.title: Ciel Fledge: A Daughter Raising Simulator
similar_games.why_similar: Another explicit daughter-raising simulator built around training choices, stat growth, bonding, and ending variation driven by your parenting decisions.

similar_games.title: Long Live the Queen
similar_games.why_similar: Also revolves around weekly planning, skill/stat development, event checks, and branching outcomes, though with more survival and political pressure than warmth.

similar_games.title: Princess Maker 5
similar_games.why_similar: Shares the core parenting loop of raising a girl over time through lessons, activities, relationship building, and ending-based progression.
